title: Book of the Dead: The Papyrus of Ani: Color Plates
source: Papyrus of Ani facsimile (Budge 1913, archive.org) + Budge translation (1895, PG #7145)
description: The Papyrus of Ani: Color Plates. From the Papyrus of Ani (c. 1250 BCE), the most famous copy of the Egyptian Book of the Dead, now in the British Museum. Facsimile pages from Budge's 1913 edition (archive.org/papyrusofanirepr01budg). Translation by E.A. Wallis Budge (1895, public domain).
introduction:
Full-color facsimile plates of the actual Papyrus of Ani (c. 1250 BCE), now in the British Museum. One of the finest surviving examples of Egyptian funerary art.

The Papyrus of Ani (c. 1250 BCE) is the most famous and most beautifully illustrated copy of the Egyptian Book of the Dead. It was made for Ani, a royal scribe of Thebes, and is now in the British Museum. The text and illustrations were first published in facsimile by E.A. Wallis Budge in 1890-1913.
